name: Bloom
summary: Adds a soft glow around bright areas in a single pass.
description: Collects thresholded neighboring samples and adds them back to the source. It is intentionally compact; multi-pass downsampled bloom will be faster and smoother at larger radii.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: threshold
uniforms.description: Luminance threshold that contributes to the glow.

uniforms.name: intensity
uniforms.description: Strength of the added glow.

uniforms.name: radius
uniforms.description: Sampling radius in source pixels.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/bloom/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("threshold", 0.62)
    shader:send("intensity", 0.85)
    shader:send("radius", 4.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Chromatic Aberration
summary: Offsets red and blue channels for a lens-like color split.
description: Samples the red and blue channels in opposite directions while retaining green from the center. It works as a subtle lens effect or a stronger impact and glitch cue.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Channel separation in source pixels.

uniforms.name: direction
uniforms.description: Direction of the channel separation.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/chromatic-aberration/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("amount", 4.0)
    shader:send("direction", {1.0, 0.25})
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Color Replace
summary: Replaces a chosen source color with a new color using a soft tolerance.
description: Measures RGB distance from a target color and blends matching pixels toward a replacement. It is a lightweight option for team colors, skin variants, and palette accents.

uniforms.name: sourceColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color to look for.

uniforms.name: replacementColor
uniforms.description: RGBA replacement color.

uniforms.name: tolerance
uniforms.description: Maximum RGB distance treated as a match.

uniforms.name: softness
uniforms.description: Width of the soft transition around the tolerance.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Overall replacement strength.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/color-replace/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("sourceColor", {0.2392, 0.8235, 0.9098, 1.0})
    shader:send("replacementColor", {1.0, 0.35, 0.6, 1.0})
    shader:send("tolerance", 0.22)
    shader:send("softness", 0.08)
    shader:send("amount", 1.0)
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: CRT
summary: Combines barrel distortion, RGB separation, scanlines, and edge falloff.
description: A compact single-pass CRT treatment intended for gameplay-sized Canvases. It deliberately favors predictable controls over physically exact display simulation.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: curvature
uniforms.description: Strength of barrel distortion.

uniforms.name: scanlineStrength
uniforms.description: Brightness modulation from scanlines.

uniforms.name: rgbOffset
uniforms.description: Horizontal RGB channel offset in source pixels.

uniforms.name: vignetteStrength
uniforms.description: Darkening near the curved edges.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/crt/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("curvature", 0.16)
    shader:send("scanlineStrength", 0.18)
    shader:send("rgbOffset", 1.4)
    shader:send("vignetteStrength", 0.48)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Directional Blur
summary: Applies a lightweight nine-tap blur along a chosen direction.
description: Samples the source along a direction vector with Gaussian-like weights. Run it twice with horizontal and vertical directions when a fuller blur is needed.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: direction
uniforms.description: Blur direction before normalization.

uniforms.name: radius
uniforms.description: Sampling radius in source pixels.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/directional-blur/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("direction", {1.0, 0.25})
    shader:send("radius", 4.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Dissolve
summary: Reveals or removes a sprite with a noisy glowing edge.
description: Uses animated value noise as a threshold mask. A colored edge follows the dissolve boundary, making the effect suitable for spawning, destruction, teleportation, and magical transitions.

uniforms.name: time
uniforms.description: Animation time in seconds.

uniforms.name: progress
uniforms.description: Dissolve progress: 0 keeps the sprite, 1 removes it.

uniforms.name: noiseScale
uniforms.description: Scale of the procedural noise pattern.

uniforms.name: softness
uniforms.description: Softness of the alpha boundary.

uniforms.name: edgeWidth
uniforms.description: Width of the colored dissolve edge.

uniforms.name: edgeColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color of the dissolve edge.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/dissolve/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("time", love.timer.getTime())
    shader:send("progress", 0.48)
    shader:send("noiseScale", 9.0)
    shader:send("softness", 0.035)
    shader:send("edgeWidth", 0.1)
    shader:send("edgeColor", {0.35, 0.95, 1.0, 1.0})
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Film Grain
summary: Adds temporally animated monochrome grain to the scene.
description: Generates deterministic screen-space noise at a configurable frame rate and applies it as luminance variation. The effect requires no noise texture.

uniforms.name: time
uniforms.description: Animation time in seconds.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Maximum grain amplitude.

uniforms.name: fps
uniforms.description: Rate at which the noise pattern changes.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/film-grain/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("time", love.timer.getTime())
    shader:send("amount", 0.1)
    shader:send("fps", 18.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Glitch
summary: Produces animated horizontal block shifts and an RGB split.
description: Selects horizontal bands with time-varying noise, shifts those bands, and offsets color channels. It is suitable for UI interruptions, damaged screens, and digital transitions.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: time
uniforms.description: Animation time in seconds.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Probability and strength of displaced bands.

uniforms.name: blockCount
uniforms.description: Approximate number of horizontal glitch bands.

uniforms.name: speed
uniforms.description: Rate at which the block pattern changes.

uniforms.name: rgbOffset
uniforms.description: Color channel offset in source pixels.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/glitch/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("time", love.timer.getTime())
    shader:send("amount", 0.42)
    shader:send("blockCount", 28.0)
    shader:send("speed", 8.0)
    shader:send("rgbOffset", 2.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Grayscale
summary: Blends a texture or scene toward perceptual grayscale.
description: Uses Rec. 709 luminance weights and an adjustable blend amount. It is useful for pause states, disabled UI, flashbacks, and selective mood changes.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Blend amount from full color to grayscale.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/grayscale/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("amount", 1.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Hit Flash
summary: Flashes a sprite toward a chosen color without changing its silhouette.
description: Blends opaque pixels toward a configurable flash color while preserving source alpha. This is a compact feedback effect for damage, pickups, and invulnerability frames.

uniforms.name: flashColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color used for the flash.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Blend amount from the source color to the flash color.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/hit-flash/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("flashColor", {1.0, 0.94, 0.72, 1.0})
    shader:send("amount", 0.78)
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Outline
summary: Adds a clean outline around transparent sprites.
description: Samples neighboring alpha values and fills transparent pixels that touch the sprite. It is useful for selection highlights, readable pixel art, and interactable objects.
notes: Keep transparent padding around the sprite so the outline has room to render.
Nearest filtering produces the crispest result for pixel art.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: outlineColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color of the outline.

uniforms.name: thickness
uniforms.description: Outline sampling distance in source pixels.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/outline/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / image:getWidth(), 1 / image:getHeight()})
    shader:send("outlineColor", {0.1059, 0.0863, 0.1804, 1.0})
    shader:send("thickness", 1.5)
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Pixelate
summary: Reduces a texture or rendered scene into configurable pixel blocks.
description: Snaps texture coordinates to the center of larger pixel cells. Apply it to a Canvas for screen-wide transitions, low-resolution simulation, or stylized censorship.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: pixelSize
uniforms.description: Block size in source pixels.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/pixelate/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / canvas:getWidth(), 1 / canvas:getHeight()})
    shader:send("pixelSize", 7.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Posterize
summary: Reduces each color channel to a configurable number of levels.
description: Quantizes RGB channels independently while preserving alpha. It creates a compact retro palette look without requiring a lookup texture.

uniforms.name: levels
uniforms.description: Number of values retained per RGB channel.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Blend amount between the source and posterized color.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/posterize/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("levels", 5.0)
    shader:send("amount", 1.0)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Radial Wipe
summary: Reveals a rendered scene outward from a configurable center point.
description: Masks the source by normalized radial distance and optionally colors the moving edge. It is a compact scene transition that requires only one Canvas.

uniforms.name: progress
uniforms.description: Reveal progress from 0 to 1.

uniforms.name: softness
uniforms.description: Softness of the reveal boundary.

uniforms.name: edgeWidth
uniforms.description: Width of the colored transition edge.

uniforms.name: center
uniforms.description: Normalized center of the radial reveal.

uniforms.name: edgeColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color of the moving edge.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/radial-wipe/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("progress", 0.62)
    shader:send("softness", 0.025)
    shader:send("edgeWidth", 0.06)
    shader:send("center", {0.5, 0.5})
    shader:send("edgeColor", {0.4, 0.96, 1.0, 1.0})
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Scanlines
summary: Adds animated horizontal scanlines to a rendered scene.
description: Modulates brightness with a screen-space sine wave and a subtle moving phase. It can be used alone or layered with other post-processing for retro display effects.

uniforms.name: time
uniforms.description: Animation time in seconds.

uniforms.name: spacing
uniforms.description: Distance between scanline peaks in screen pixels.

uniforms.name: strength
uniforms.description: Maximum brightness reduction.

uniforms.name: speed
uniforms.description: Vertical phase animation speed.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/scanlines/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("time", love.timer.getTime())
    shader:send("spacing", 3.0)
    shader:send("strength", 0.28)
    shader:send("speed", 1.3)
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Silhouette
summary: Recolors a sprite to a solid tint while preserving alpha detail.
description: Replaces RGB values with a single configurable color and leaves the source alpha intact. It works for shadows, hidden enemies, interaction highlights, and monochrome UI icons.

uniforms.name: silhouetteColor
uniforms.description: RGBA silhouette color.

uniforms.name: amount
uniforms.description: Blend amount between the source and silhouette.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/silhouette/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("silhouetteColor", {0.15, 0.07, 0.3, 0.88})
    shader:send("amount", 1.0)
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Vignette
summary: Darkens or tints the edges of a rendered scene.
description: Builds an aspect-correct radial mask around the screen center. Radius, softness, intensity, and tint are adjustable for subtle focus or dramatic tunnel vision.

uniforms.name: aspect
uniforms.description: Source width divided by source height.

uniforms.name: radius
uniforms.description: Distance from center where the vignette begins.

uniforms.name: softness
uniforms.description: Width of the vignette transition.

uniforms.name: intensity
uniforms.description: Blend strength of the vignette color.

uniforms.name: vignetteColor
uniforms.description: RGBA color applied at the edges.

usageSource:
-- Draw your scene to a Canvas first.
local canvas = love.graphics.newCanvas()

local function drawScene()
    -- Draw the game world here.
end

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/vignette/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("aspect", canvas:getWidth() / canvas:getHeight())
    shader:send("radius", 0.68)
    shader:send("softness", 0.38)
    shader:send("intensity", 0.72)
    shader:send("vignetteColor", {0.0353, 0.0196, 0.0902, 1.0})
end

function love.draw()
    love.graphics.setCanvas(canvas)
    love.graphics.clear()
    drawScene()
    love.graphics.setCanvas()

    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(canvas)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end

name: Wave
summary: Distorts a sprite with a smooth animated two-axis wave.
description: Offsets texture coordinates with sine and cosine waves. The amplitude is expressed in source pixels, so the effect remains predictable across texture sizes.

uniforms.name: texelSize
uniforms.description: Reciprocal width and height of the source texture.

uniforms.name: time
uniforms.description: Animation time in seconds.

uniforms.name: amplitude
uniforms.description: Maximum displacement in source pixels.

uniforms.name: frequency
uniforms.description: Number of wave oscillations across the texture.

uniforms.name: speed
uniforms.description: Animation speed.

usageSource:
-- Assume `image` is a loaded love.graphics.Image.

local shader = love.graphics.newShader("shaders/wave/shader.glsl")

local function updateShader()
    shader:send("texelSize", {1 / image:getWidth(), 1 / image:getHeight()})
    shader:send("time", love.timer.getTime())
    shader:send("amplitude", 3.0)
    shader:send("frequency", 18.0)
    shader:send("speed", 2.2)
end

function love.draw()
    updateShader()
    love.graphics.setShader(shader)
    love.graphics.draw(image, 100, 100)
    love.graphics.setShader()
end
